Just to pick you back off the reason close, you're going to want to add a satisfaction guarantee on top of that. And that will help you close even more sales. It'll also make you a compliant advertiser. So essentially, like I'm telling you, when you offer satisfaction guarantees, you will close far more sales than you will ever lose on the back end because people will take that extra step because they feel like they have no risk. Now that being said, if you provide exceptional service, you literally have nothing to worry about. When we ran these, I always had a satisfaction guarantee based on the level of service. And listen, if you feel like you don't get the level of service that you are paying for, I will give you the money back myself, independent of whether you hit the goal. And that's if you feel like we didn't deliver the service. And that's how confident I am. And I would even use this in the close. I'd be like, I've been offering that guarantee for the last five years, and I've had two people take me up on it. And that's true. I've only had two people ever take me up on it. So you can use that. And if this is the first time that you have been using it, then you can very truthfully say you've had no one ever take you up on it. So you can use that to your advantage. But make sure that you mention that. And just as a general practice, just everyone, I know we just went through the sale stuff and it's exciting. It's awesome. But I just want to take a quick pivot back and just understand that if anyone is going to ever ask for their money back, just give it to them. It's just good business practice. It's not worth the attention. It's not worth the headache. It's not worth the negative press. It's not worth the negative reviews. It's not worth the negative reputation. It's just not worth it. If someone is upset, just give them their money back. And whether that's independent from this program. I'm just saying in general, if you got some person who hasn't come for four months, you know what I mean? And they say, hey, give me the last month back. Just give it to them. You know what I mean? It's just not worth the time. It's not worth the attention. Just focus on getting more customers and helping and just helping people. And when you operate from that standpoint, it will help you in your sale because you'll always feel like you have an absolute clear conscience when you're selling because you can just be like, listen, best case, worst case, best case scenario, absolutely change your life and you have this money that you can credit forward to making a permanent change. Worst case scenario, you get to the end of this. And if you think we sucked, I'll give your money back. So that's the best case, worst case. And the middle case is you lose 18 pounds. You just fall a little bit short of the goal and we still give you X percent towards staying anyways because we want to reward you for making the change. And would you still be happy with losing 18 pounds? Would you still be happy if you lost 5 percent? You know what I mean? For X period of time, well, yeah, okay, cool. Because then on top of that, you're still going to get this huge discount on the back. So it's just making them understand that. But you need to make sure definitely add in the satisfaction guarantee. You will close way more sales. You'll close with conviction because you know genuinely that they have nothing to lose. And that it will help them take that last step for those people who are fearful. And yeah, just 100 percent always honor it, always be, just always follow through. So hope that makes sense. That is the last piece. It's also reflected on all the contracts and whatnot anyways. So just just stack it as value in the sale and you'll close way more people and help more people just get out of their own way.
